qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated REST provider API allows arbitrary routing configuration injection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: While this finding is in the same repository (Traefik), it describes a different vulnerability: missing authentication on the REST provider API endpoint allowing arbitrary routing configuration injection. The advisory describes forwarded-header alias spoofing (underscore variants like X_Forwarded_Proto bypassing sanitization) leading to authentication bypass. These have different root causes: this finding is about missing authentication on a REST API, while the advisory is about incomplete header sanitization in authentication middleware.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only qualifying finding does not match the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory (GHSA-5m6w-wvh7-57vm) describes a pre-authentication decision bypass in Traefik's ForwardAuth middleware due to incomplete forwarded-header sanitization that fails to strip alias variants using underscores (e.g., X_Forwarded_Proto). The 'rest-provider-unauthenticated-config-injection' finding describes a different Traefik vulnerability - missing authentication on the REST provider API - which has a completely different root cause (missing authentication guard on an API endpoint) compared to the advisory's root cause (incomplete header sanitization in authentication middleware).
evidence: Advisory describes forwarded-header alias spoofing (underscore variants bypassing sanitization) in Traefik ForwardAuth middleware
Advisory references: pkg/middlewares/forwardedheaders/forwarded_header.go for canonical header handling
Finding 'rest-provider-unauthenticated-config-injection' is about missing authentication on REST provider API - different root cause (missing auth guard vs incomplete header sanitization)
